name: Chocolate Cherry Torte 01- Country Living
recipeIngredient: Chocolate Meringue Twigs: see recipe below
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare Dried Cherry Filling: In a small saucepan, combine dried cherries, water, and 3 tbsp sugar. Bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to low and simmer until the cherries are soft and the liquid is reduced by half. Remove from heat and transfer half of the cherry mixture to a blender or food processor; process into a chunky puree. Return pureed mixture to saucepan with remaining cherry mixture, cornstarch, and stir until well blended. Heat to boiling, stirring constantly, then cook until thickened. Remove from heat and stir in 3 tbsp Marsala wine and lemon juice. Cool to room temperature.
2. Prepare Coffee Syrup: Combine coffee with the remaining 3 tbsp sugar and 3 tbsp Marsala wine; set aside.
3. Prepare Chocolate Cake: In a small bowl, stir boiling water and cocoa powder until smooth. Set aside to cool to room temperature.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 15-1/2 by 10-1/2-inch jelly-roll pan and line with waxed paper; grease and flour the paper.
5. In a large bowl, beat sugar and shortening until fluffy. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in vanilla and cooled cocoa mixture.
6. Into a small bowl, sift together cake flour, salt, baking powder, and baking soda. Alternately add the flour mixture with buttermilk to the shortening mixture, beating until batter is smooth. Spread batter evenly in prepared pan and bake for 18-20 minutes or until center springs back when gently pressed. Cool cake in pan on a wire rack for 10 minutes. Invert cake onto a wire rack; remove waxed paper and cool completely.
7. Prepare Cream-Cheese Filling: In a large bowl, beat cream cheese and confectioners' sugar with an electric mixer until well combined (2 minutes). In a small bowl, beat heavy cream to soft peaks. Gently fold whipped cream into the cream-cheesecake mixture until just blended. Do not over-mix.
Chocolate Meringue Twigs:
(Recipe follows)
- Egg Whites: 3 large
- Granulated Sugar: 1 cup
Beat egg whites with an electric mixer until foamy. Gradually add granulated sugar while continuing to beat. Once stiff peaks form, pipe twirls onto waxed paper or parchment paper. Bake at 250°F (130°C) for 15 minutes or until crisp and dry. Cool completely before using.
Decorative Ribbon:
(Option)
- Use a decorative ribbon to tie around the center of the cake or to create a lace border on top.
